steps.title: Find the singular subject
steps.content: The subject of the verb is survey, not plants or species. The long phrase after survey only describes what was surveyed, so the verb must agree with the singular noun survey.

steps.title: Choose singular past tense
steps.content: A and C are present-tense forms, and A, B, and C are plural forms that do not agree with the singular subject. The singular past-tense verb fits the subject and the historical context. The answer is D.
